originalTitle: Decoding the obfuscated bash script on a Uniqlo t-shirt
content:
When my wife said to me “Let me show you a t-shirt I saw…”, I wasn’t sure what to expect, but it definitely wasn’t an obfuscated bash script printed on the back designed to print a happy Easter egg message.

I’m not in the habit of clickbaity headlines I’ve no idea at all how many views this site gets, but I’m willing to bet it’s not even double-digit humans per month. but I can see why subeditors have such fun with them. The title above is, strictly speaking, entirely accurate, but probably not what you think. The obfuscated code in question is actually an easter egg, it’s being supplied via Uniqlo stores on an excellent t-shirt designed by Akamai in support of their Peace for All campaign .

And it’s very cool! The front has a heart in curly braces:

While the back has a big alphanumeric block:

My wife was right that I’d want to see it. Was that… a shebang?

Take a closer look at the text block:

Yes, a shebang! On a t-shirt sold in a high street store, no less. And it is clearly a base64 encoded Here string being fed to eval via base64 --decode .

Interesting. I told my wife "that’s basically how people ship viruses’ and bought it.

I ran OCR in a few ways: First, using the built-in OCR of the circle-to-search feature on Android, which is often very good. Second, by using Tesseract with a few options and tweaks. And third by running it through Claude. After diffing the three to look for mismatches and getting Claude to output a table of locations for quick scanning, it became trivial but time-consuimg to tidy up the remainder. The resulting string was:

After Base64 decoding, the resulting script is a welcoming and nicely commented Easter egg:

The result is a continuous happy sine-wave loop of the campaign message, Peace for All:

Edit: The following font ID is wrong! User ralphinus on Hacker News pointed out that the font is Roboto Mono. I don’t know how I overlooked the very different g .

I guess Uniqlo is run through Windows though: one thing that struck me was the font, which I’m I was previously almost certain is Consolas I was fortunate enough to correspond with the designer, Lucas de Groot, once in relation to a legal case in which someone had used one of his fonts to forge a document. He was very helpful and kind enough to confirm the necessary facts in writing, even though he owed nothing to us. , which I’m fond of. Note the very shallowly-slashed 0 , the lack of serif on the 1 and the rounded curves of letters like BDyg and number 2 . It’s striking because it’s primarily a Windows font, so not the sort of thing I’d expect to see calling Bash.

Linux, the language of the Internet

Akamai put out a press release about the shirt when it was released, which is another sort of interesting due to the blend of tech and marketing:

More than 25 years ago, Akamai helped make the internet we know today possible. This shirt’s design is a callback to those early days of life online. The light tan color is a reference to the “beige box” plastic casings that housed the early internet computers, and the heart on the front represents how the internet has been used for good all over the world. On the back of the T-shirt is real code. It’s a reference to Linux, the open-source language of the internet. This common language unites Akamai with the world’s top brands and the people they serve, as we work together toward a vision of a safer and more connected world.

I deliberately didn’t search for spoilers at first, but I see that I am of course not the first person to get nerd-sniped by this. Wen Chuan Lee and that post also links to another (against which I’ve cross-checked my transcription above). I’m happy to carry on the chain.

Read the discussion of this post on Hacker News, which includes links to more info from the designer and other interesting observations
chineseTitle: 解码优衣库 T 恤上混淆的 bash 脚本
chineseContent:
当我的妻子对我说“让我给你看一件我看到的 T 恤……”时，我不确定会发生什么，但这绝对不是印在背面的混淆的 bash 脚本，旨在打印快乐的复活节彩蛋信息。

我不习惯点击标题，我根本不知道这个网站有多少浏览量，但我敢打赌，每月浏览量甚至不会达到两位数。但我能理解为什么副编辑对它们如此感兴趣。严格来说，上面的标题是完全准确的，但可能不是你想象的那样。所讨论的混淆代码实际上是一个复活节彩蛋，它通过优衣库商店在 Akamai 设计的一件精美 T 恤上提供，以支持他们的“人人享有和平”活动。

这非常酷！前面有一个用花括号括起来的心形：

背面有一个大字母数字块：

我妻子说得对，我想看看。那是……一个shebang吗？

仔细看看文本块：

是的，一个shebang！大街商店出售的 T 恤上也同样如此。它显然是一个 Base64 编码的 Here 字符串，通过 base64 --decode 馈送到 eval 。

有趣的。我告诉我的妻子“这基本上就是人们传播病毒的方式”并购买了它。

我通过几种方式运行 OCR：首先，使用 Android 上圈搜索功能的内置 OCR，这通常非常好。其次，通过使用 Tesseract 进行一些选项和调整。第三，通过克劳德来运行它。在对三者进行比较以查找不匹配并让克劳德输出位置表以进行快速扫描之后，整理其余部分变得微不足道但非常耗时。结果字符串是：

经过 Base64 解码后，生成的脚本是一个受欢迎且评论良好的复活节彩蛋：

结果是竞选信息“人人享有和平”的连续快乐正弦波循环：

编辑：以下字体ID错误！ Hacker News 上的用户 ralphinus 指出，该字体是 Roboto Mono。我不知道我是如何忽略了截然不同的 g 的。

我想优衣库是通过 Windows 运行的：令我印象深刻的是字体，我之前几乎可以肯定是 Consolas。我很幸运能够与设计师 Lucas de Groot 通信，有一次涉及一个法律案件，其中有人使用他的一种字体伪造文档。他非常乐于助人，而且很友善，以书面形式确认了必要的事实，尽管他不欠我们任何东西。 ，我很喜欢。请注意非常浅的斜线 0 、 1 上没有衬线以及 BDyg 和数字 2 等字母的圆形曲线。它很引人注目，因为它主要是一种 Windows 字体，所以不是我期望看到的调用 Bash 的那种东西。

Linux，互联网语言

Akamai 在这款 T 恤发布时发布了一份新闻稿，由于技术和营销的结合，这又是另一种有趣的内容：

25 多年前，Akamai 帮助我们打造了今天的互联网。这件衬衫的设计让人回想起早期的网络生活。浅棕褐色是指容纳早期互联网计算机的“米色盒子”塑料外壳，正面的心形代表互联网如何在世界各地得到良好利用。 T恤背面有真实代码。它指的是互联网的开源语言 Linux。这种共同语言将 Akamai 与世界顶级品牌及其服务的人们团结在一起，共同努力实现更安全、更互联的世界的愿景。

一开始我故意不去剧透，但我发现我当然不是第一个因此而被书呆子狙击的人。 Wen Chuan Lee 和那篇文章还链接到另一篇文章（我已经交叉检查了上面的转录）。我很高兴能带着这条链子。

阅读 Hacker News 上这篇文章的讨论，其中包括来自设计者的更多信息和其他有趣观察的链接
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
